pairs.question: What was the original medium for the Bamboozle! quiz game?
pairs.answer: Bamboozle! was originally a quiz game featured on Channel 4 Teletext in the United Kingdom, specifically under the 'Fun & Games' category.

pairs.question: Why did Bamboozle! stop being a real-time game synchronized with TV broadcasts?
pairs.answer: In 1993, the new broadcast teletext franchise holders (Teletext UK) opted for X.25 packet switching, which made it impossible to adequately synchronize the broadcast of teletext content with a TV programme, leading the game to revert to its 1994 format.

pairs.question: What was the theme of the last edition of Bamboozle! on Teletext, and when did it appear?
pairs.answer: The last edition of Bamboozle! on Teletext was themed around 'Ends and Lasts' and appeared on Monday, 14 December 2009.

pairs.question: Where was Bamboozle! re-released after Teletext's discontinuation, and in what year?
pairs.answer: Bamboozle! was re-released on the iPhone by Teletext Ltd. on 9 August 2010, featuring all the retro graphics.

pairs.question: How did Teletext Holidays adapt Bamboozle! in 2019?
pairs.answer: Teletext Holidays launched a version of the Bamboozle quiz on their 404 error page on 11 July 2019.

pairs.question: What was the format of Bamboozle! inspired by, and which TV show used a similar format?
pairs.answer: Bamboozle! used a multiple-choice format, similar to the TV show 'Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?'.

pairs.question: What message did the Boozler 'family' convey on their final appearance?
pairs.answer: The Boozler 'family' appeared one last time on Tuesday, 15 December 2009, to say farewell to the Teletext audience.
